classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
To Brunichild, Queen of the Franks.

ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Brunichild, Queen of the Franks.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Brunichild, &c.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now that your Excellency’s royal solicitude is in all matters of government praiseworthy, you ought, for the increase of your glory, to show yourself more watchful, and careful not to allow those whom you rule with counsel outwardly to perish inwardly among themselves.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: So may you, through the fruit of your pious solicitude, after occupying this topmost height of a temporal kingdom, attain under God to kingdoms and joys that are eternal.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And this we trust you will be able after the following manner to succeed in; if, among other good deeds, you pay attention to the ordination of priests; whose office, as we have learnt, has come in your parts to be such an object of ambition that priests are ordained all at once from being laymen.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: This is a very serious matter.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For what can they effect, what good can they do the people, who covet being made bishops, not for doing good, but for distinction?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: These, then, who have not yet learnt what they have to teach—what do they effect, but that the unlawful advancement of a few becomes the ruin of many, and that the observance of ecclesiastical government is brought into confusion, seeing that no regular order is observed?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For whoso comes to the control thereof inconsiderately and hurriedly, with what admonition can he edify those who are put under him, his example having taught them, not reason, but error?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: It is a shame in truth, it is a shame, for one to command others what he knows not how to observe himself.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Nor do we pass over that other thing which in like manner requires amendment, but detest it as utterly execrable and a most serious matter; that in your parts sacred orders are conferred through simoniacal heresy, which was the first to arise against the Church, and was condemned with a rigorous malediction.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence, therefore, it is brought about that the dignity of the priesthood comes into contempt, and holy honour under condemnation.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so reverence perishes, discipline is destroyed, since he who ought to have corrected faults committed them; and by nefarious ambition the honourable priesthood is brought under censure and disparagement.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For who will any more venerate what is sold, or not think worthless what is bought?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence I am greatly distressed, and condole with that land; since, while they scorn to have as a divine gift, but compass by bribes, the Holy Spirit which Almighty God deigns to bestow on men through the imposition of hands, I do not think that the priesthood can long subsist there.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For where the gifts of heavenly grace are sold, the life is not sought for God’s service, but rather money is venerated in opposition to God.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Seeing then that so great a wickedness is not only a danger to them, but also in no small degree injurious to your kingdom, greeting your Excellency with fatherly affection we beseech you to make God propitious to you by the correction of this enormity.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, that there may be henceforth no opportunity of committing it, let a synod be held by your order, at which, in the presence of our most beloved son, the abbot Cyriacus, it shall be interdicted strictly under pain of anathema that any one should dare to pass suddenly from a lay condition to the degree of the Episcopate, or any one whatever dare to give or receive anything for ecclesiastical orders; that so our Lord and Redeemer may so deal with the things that are yours as He shall see you to be solicitous with pious devotion in the things that are His.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But we have taken special care to delegate the charge and management of this synod, which we have decided should be held, to our brother and fellow-bishop Syagrius, whom we know to be peculiarly your own; and we beg you to deign both to lend a willing ear to his supplication, and to support him by your aid; to the end that what may redound to your reward, namely a pious and God-pleasing ordination of priests, the contagion of this evil being removed, may take effect within all the limits of your jurisdiction.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: To this our brother, in that he has shewn himself exceedingly devoted with regard to the mission which has been sent, under God, to the nation of the Angli, we have sent a pallium to be used in the solemnities of mass, so that, having given aid in things spiritual, he may find himself advanced by the favour of the Prince of the apostles in the spiritual order itself.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Furthermore, we have altogether wondered why in your kingdom you allow Jews to possess Christian slaves.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For what are all Christians but members of Christ?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And we all know that you sincerely honour the Head of these members.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But let your Excellency consider how inconsistent it is to honour the Head and to allow the members to be trampled on by his enemies.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so we beg that your Excellency’s ordinance may remove the mischief of this iniquity from your kingdom; so that you may prove yourself the more to be a worthy worshipper of Almighty God, in that you set his faithful ones free from His enemies.
